title: Agent Heartbeat
summary: Wake an agent on a regular interval. What happens each heartbeat is up to the agent's other active Epics.
description: A scheduler-oriented Epic that owns the wake-up cadence and nothing else. It does not prescribe what work the agent performs — other active Epics and standing instructions determine that. This Epic ensures the agent wakes on time and logs that it did.
skillMd:
# SKILL.md

## Purpose

Wake the agent on a regular interval. What happens each heartbeat is determined by the agent's other active Epics and standing instructions — this Epic only owns the cadence.

## Operating loop

1. Wake on schedule. Read `state.json` for cadence and last run time.
2. Hand control to the agent to act on whatever is current.
3. When the agent finishes its work, write a log entry to `log/`.
4. Update `state.json` with the completed run time and next scheduled wake.

## Rules

- This Epic manages the clock, not the work.
- Every wake must update `state.json` so the next interval is known.
- If the agent has nothing to do, log that and schedule the next wake anyway.
epicMd:
# EPIC.md

## Objective

Provide a durable wake-up cadence so the agent can act on its own schedule without manual prompting. This Epic does not prescribe what the agent does — only that it wakes up.

## Phases

1. **Configure** — set the cadence and record it in `state.json`.
2. **Run** — wake on schedule, hand control to the agent, record that a heartbeat occurred.

## Success criteria

- Heartbeats fire at the configured cadence.
- Every heartbeat is logged regardless of whether the agent had work to do.
- `state.json` always reflects the last and next scheduled wake.

## State to preserve

- `state.json` — cadence, last run time, next run time
- `log/` — one entry per heartbeat

## Guardrails

- Do not decide what work to do — that belongs to other Epics.
- Do not skip a scheduled wake without logging why.

## Resume

Read `state.json` for the cadence and last run time. Compute whether a heartbeat is overdue. Read the last 3–5 `log/` entries to confirm recent wake history. Resume the cadence from the current interval.
features: Configurable wake-up cadence independent of what work gets done
Logs every heartbeat whether or not the agent had work to do
Persistent state tracking last run, next run, and cadence
